name: La Via Augusta por la Plana Baja — Circular desde Villareal
name_original: La Vía Augusta por la Plana Baja — Circular desde Villareal
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Vila-Real offer diverse terrain, transitioning from coastal plains to the foothills of the Sierra de Espadán. The region is characterized by extensive orange groves, the meandering Mijares River, and varied elevation gains. Cyclists can find routes that explore the flatlands or challenge themselves with climbs into the nearby mountain ranges. This area provides a compelling destination for road cyclists seeking varied landscapes in the province of Castellón. Best road cycling routes around Vila-Real The most popular road cycling route is La Via Augusta por la Plana Baja — Circular desde Villareal, a 29.4 miles (47.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 58 minutes to complete. This easy route offers a circular path through the local plains. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Aín Village – Coll d’Aín loop from Vila-real, a moderate 43.9 miles (70.6 km) path. This route includes significant elevation gain, leading through the scenic Coll d’Aín. Local road cyclists also love the Eslida Pass – Eslida Pass loop from Vila-real, a 66.2 miles (106.5 km) trail leading through the challenging Eslida Pass, often completed in about 5 hours 26 minutes. Road cycling around Vila-Real is defined by the foothills of the Sierra de Espadán, the Mijares River, and fragrant orange groves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from gentle rides to challenging climbs. The routes in Vila-Real are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 4000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Vila-Real's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Vila-Real?
A: Vila-Real offers a diverse network of over 50 road cycling routes. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy rides through the plains to challenging climbs in the foothills of the Sierra de Espadán.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Vila-Real?
A: Road cycling in Vila-Real features varied terrain, transitioning from flat coastal plains and extensive orange groves to the rolling foothills of the Sierra de Espadán. You'll find routes that follow the Mijares River, offering gentle gradients, as well as more challenging climbs with significant elevation gains.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy ride?
A: Yes, Vila-Real has several easy road cycling routes. For instance, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e883468357/la-via-augusta-por-la-plana-baja-circular-desde-villareal" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Via Augusta por la Plana Baja — Circular desde Villareal&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 29.4-mile (47.2 km) circular path that offers a relaxed exploration through the local plains with minimal elevation gain. There are 13 easy routes in total.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling climbs near Vila-Real?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24396077" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eslida Pass – Eslida Pass loop from Vila-real&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gains, leading through the demanding Eslida Pass. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24439059" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aín Village – Coll d’Aín loop from Vila-real&lt;/a&gt;, which includes a substantial climb through the scenic Coll d’Aín.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Vila-Real?
A: Many road cycling routes in Vila-Real are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e883468357/la-via-augusta-por-la-plana-baja-circular-desde-villareal" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Via Augusta por la Plana Baja — Circular desde Villareal&lt;/a&gt;, which provides an easy circular path through the local plains.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Vila-Real?
A: The most popular months for road cycling in Vila-Real are typically May and December. These periods generally offer favorable weather conditions for exploring the region's diverse landscapes, from coastal areas to mountain foothills.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by water features like reservoirs or rivers?
A: Yes, several routes in Vila-Real incorporate water features. The Mijares River meanders through the region, and many cycling paths follow its course. You can also find routes that pass by reservoirs, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1674888" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sichar Reservoir – Sitjar Dam loop from Vila-real&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5174600" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sichar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3056707" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sitjar Dam&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other cyclists say about the road cycling routes in Vila-Real?
A: The road cycling routes in Vila-Real are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from the fragrant orange groves to the challenging climbs of the Sierra de Espadán, and the well-maintained paths.

Q: Are there options for longer distance road cycling trails near Vila-Real?
A: Yes, Vila-Real offers routes suitable for longer distances. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24396077" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eslida Pass – Eslida Pass loop from Vila-real&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 66.2-mile (106.5 km) trail, providing an extended ride through the region's challenging passes.

Q: Are there less crowded road bike trails in Vila-Real?
A: While specific 'less crowded' routes aren't explicitly highlighted, the diverse network of over 50 routes means you can often find quieter options, especially outside of peak times. Exploring some of the moderate or difficult routes that venture into the Sierra de Espadán foothills might offer a more secluded experience compared to the more popular, flatter routes.

Q: Can I combine road cycling with exploring local attractions or viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. Many road cycling routes in Vila-Real offer opportunities to see local landmarks and natural features. You can cycle towards the foothills of the Sierra de Espadán, where you might encounter peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4760025" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penya Migdia&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4904502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Puntal&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views. Routes also pass through charming local areas and extensive orange groves.

Q: Are there routes that offer a mix of coastal and inland scenery?
A: Yes, Vila-Real's location allows for routes that transition from agricultural plains to the Mediterranean coastline. You can experience the unique blend of fragrant orange groves and riverside scenery along the Mijares River, eventually extending towards the beach for a diverse cycling experience.

Q: Are there any routes that specifically pass through the famous orange groves?
A: The flatlands surrounding Vila-Real are characterized by extensive orange groves, and many gentler rides will take you directly through these visually appealing and fragrant landscapes. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e883468357/la-via-augusta-por-la-plana-baja-circular-desde-villareal" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Via Augusta por la Plana Baja — Circular desde Villareal&lt;/a&gt; are excellent for experiencing this iconic regional scenery.

Q: What are some moderate difficulty road cycling routes in the area?
A: Vila-Real offers 29 moderate difficulty routes. A good example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19877940" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marianet Pass – Eslida Pass loop from Vila-real&lt;/a&gt;, a 73.1-mile (73.1 km) route with significant elevation gain, providing a balanced challenge for intermediate cyclists.
tips: The Via Augusta is a historical path that the Romans traveled and has been known for a long time. Today it runs for 1500 km crossing several countries along the Mediterranean coast. In many sectors it is paved and suitable for any type of bicycle. A very picturesque road with a lot of history behind it.
